<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Note description</h4><a id="user-content-note-description" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Note description" href="#note-description"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The sing line is defined so that it has the <code>NoteType</code>, <code>StartBeat</code>, <code>Lenght</code>, <code>Pitch</code> and <code>Text</code>.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>For the syles see <a href="#noteTypes">NoteTypes</a>.</li>
<li>The <code>StartBeat</code> and <code>Lenght</code> must be calculated against the <code>BPM</code>, <code>GAP</code> and <code>Relative</code>. Is a beat number.</li>
<li>The pitch describes the note as a number. The number 0 corresponds to the note C4 and Midi Note 60.</li>
<li>Text is the part of the lyrics that is sung in this note.</li>
</ul>

<p dir="auto">Specifies the delay in milliseconds between the start of the video and the start of the song.
This value can be used to synchronize the video with the music.
This attribute is useful when the video and audio files are not perfectly synchronized and you need to adjust the timing of the video to match the audio. By setting a positive or negative value for VIDEOGAP, you can delay or advance the start of the video relative to the start of the song.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">RELATIVE</h3><a id="user-content-relative" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: RELATIVE" href="#relative"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Specifies whether the note timings in the file are relative to the previous note or absolute. If this attribute is set to yes, the note timings are relative to the previous note. If it is set to no or not present, the note timings are absolute.
If this line is missing, then the timestamps are absolute.</p>

<p dir="auto">Relative timestamps make working on a TXT file much easier, because if you want to insert a pause, you don't have to adjust all following timestamps, but only the ones until the end of the line.
If you now want to change from absolute to relative timestamps yourself, this means a lot of manual work.</p>
